articles.title: IMD issues heatwave alert for 10 Odisha districts; temperature likely to rise further - Odisha TV
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Odisha
articles.full_text:
The sweltering heat continues to grip Odisha, with the India Meteorological Department (IMD) warning of a further rise in day temperatures by 2 to 3 degrees Celsius over the next two days.
The Regional Meteorological Centre in Bhubaneswar has issued a heatwave warning for several districts in western and interior parts of the state. The districts under alert include Bolangir, Bargarh, Jharsuguda, Sundargarh, Deogarh, Angul, Boudh, Kandhamal, Sonepur, and Sambalpur.
In addition to the heatwave conditions, coastal districts are expected to experience intense humidity and discomfort over the next seven days, making weather conditions increasingly oppressive.
On Thursday, at least six towns in Odisha recorded temperatures above 40 degrees Celsius. Titilagarh emerged as the hottest place in the state, registering a maximum temperature of 42 degrees Celsius.
“The temperature is likely to increase by 2 to 3 degree Celsius in the coming days. Humid conditions are likely to prevail in the coastal districts for the next seven days. Residents of Bolangir, Bargarh, Jharsuguda, Sundargarh, Deogarh, Angul, Boudh, Kandhamal, and Sonepur are advised to take precautionary measures in view of the heatwave condition,” said Manorama Mohanty, Bhubaneswar met centre director.

The weather office has advised residents to take necessary precautions, avoid prolonged exposure to the sun, and stay hydrated as the heatwave conditions are likely to persist.

articles.title: IMD Issues Yellow Warning for Heatwave and Thunderstorms in Odisha - Dailyhunt
articles.full_text:
Bhubaneswar, 15 April (H.S.): The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a series of weather alerts for Odisha, warning of heatwave conditions and thunderstorm activity over the next few days.
According to the IMD, hot and humid conditions are expected to prevail from Today to April 18 across several districts, including Balasore, Bhadrak, Jajpur, Kendrapara, Cuttack, Jagatsinghpur, Puri, Khurda, Nayagarh, Ganjam, and Gajapati. These conditions are likely to impact daily life and increase the risk of heat-related illnesses.
For April 16 and 17, heatwave conditions are likely in districts such as Bolangir, Jharsuguda, Sambalpur, and Sonepur. At the same time, thunderstorms accompanied by lightning and gusty winds (30-40 kmph) are expected in parts of Mayurbhanj, Keonjhar, and Balasore.
By April 18, light to moderate rainfall or thunderstorms are likely at isolated places in southern and central districts, including Rayagada, Koraput, and Malkangiri, which may bring some relief from the prevailing heat. However, thunderstorm activity with gusty winds is expected to continue.
The IMD further indicated that heatwave conditions may extend to districts like Sundergarh, Bargarh, and Angul in the coming days, with heat warnings likely to remain in place until April 21.
---------------
Hindusthan Samachar / Monalisa Panda

articles.title: Heatwave alert issued for 10 Odisha districts as temperatures set to rise - ODISHA RAY
articles.full_text:
Heatwave alert issued for 10 Odisha districts as temperatures set to rise
Bhubaneswar (16/04/2026): By Ishani Chhatoraj: The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a heatwave alert for 10 districts in Odisha, warning of a further rise in day temperatures over the next two days. According to the weather agency, maximum temperatures across the state are likely to increase by 2 to 3 degrees Celsius, intensifying the already prevailing hot and humid conditions.Several districts are expected to experience severe heatwave conditions, posing health risks, especially to vulnerable groups. The IMD has also issued a yellow warning for both heatwave and thunderstorm activities in parts of the state.While the heat continues to dominate most regions, isolated areas may witness thunderstorms accompanied by gusty winds, offering brief but limited relief. Simultaneously, light thunderstorms and rain are expected in coastal areas like Kalahandi and Cuttack. Residents have been advised to take precautionary measures, including avoiding direct exposure to the sun during peak hours, staying hydrated, and limiting outdoor activities.Authorities are closely monitoring the situation and may issue further advisories if conditions worsen. With temperatures steadily climbing, Odisha remains on alert as the state braces for more intense summer conditions in the coming days. Heatwave Alert: A heatwave warning is in effect for 10 districts: Bolangir, Bargarh, Jharsuguda, Sundargarh, Deogarh, Angul, Boudh, Kandhamal, Sonepur, and Sambalpur. High Temperatures: Several towns recorded over 40 degrees Celsius, with Titilagarh reaching 42 degrees Celsius on April 15, 2026. Humidity Warning: Coastal districts, including Bhubaneswar, will likely experience high discomfort and humid conditions for the next seven days. Nowcast Alert: Earlier, light to moderate thunderstorms with lightning and light rain were reported in parts of Kalahandi, Rayagada, Gajapati, Khurda, Cuttack, and Jajpur.
